assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Sofia, I see Series A closed three weeks ago and the investor has already asked for cleaner contractor controls before the next diligence. Mellow acts as Contractor of Record with a clean audit trail on every payment — documents and KYC that can be shown to investors. We don't promise to remove your legal risk — we remove the unstructured layer before the next round. Does this fit your context?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.

criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Sofia, I see Series A closed three weeks ago and the investor has already asked for cleaner contractor controls before the next diligence. Mellow acts as Contractor of Record with a clean audit trail on every payment — documents and KYC that can be shown to investors. objections.objection: You noticed the round. So directly: what part of contractor documentation do you cover, and what stays with me?
The next round isn't tomorrow. Why should I change the scheme now if the current one hasn't blown up?
Fine. Send the flow and audit story — brief, no fluff. If it's real, we can talk for 20 minutes.
Waiting for the materials. When will you send them?

criteria.label: Core objection handling

criteria.name: Next step
criteria.evidence_quote: The current setup stops being defensible in two scenarios: a payment gets stuck with no SLA path — or diligence comes and there's no audit trail. Your current setup has both risks. Mellow removes them specifically. What's most important to you heading into the next step?
criteria.label: Clear next step

transcript.text: Sofia, I see Series A closed three weeks ago and the investor has already asked for cleaner contractor controls before the next diligence. Mellow acts as Contractor of Record with a clean audit trail on every payment — documents and KYC that can be shown to investors. We don't promise to remove your legal risk — we remove the unstructured layer before the next round. Does this fit your context?
You noticed the round. So directly: what part of contractor documentation do you cover, and what stays with me?
On SLA: a named escalation owner, defined response time in the contract, incident trail documented. Not general support — a process with accountability.
The next round isn't tomorrow. Why should I change the scheme now if the current one hasn't blown up?

The current setup stops being defensible in two scenarios: a payment gets stuck with no SLA path — or diligence comes and there's no audit trail. Your current setup has both risks. Mellow removes them specifically. What's most important to you heading into the next step?
Fine. Send the flow and audit story — brief, no fluff. If it's real, we can talk for 20 minutes.
I'll send you a one-pager: flow, SLA, and how the audit trail looks before diligence. If it speaks to your situation — let's do a short call.
Waiting for the materials. When will you send them?
Let me send you a brief written summary: payment flow, audit story, and scope boundary. If it's concrete and grounded, can we find 20 minutes this week?
